content.problemStatement:
Steps to if a word exists in a trie:  
- set a variable current and initialize it with root of the trie.
- iterate over the word to search in the trie.
    - update the current variable to the pointer stored in the children array at the position of the index of the character of the word.
- if the current node is not NULL and it's end is set to true then the word exists in the trie, else it doesn't exist in the trie.

Let's follow these steps to search if the word "cats" exists in the following trie:

```
      [ c     d ]
       /       \
     [a]       [o]
     /           \
  [ l  t ]       [g]
   /    \          \
 [l]    [[s],end]  [end]
 /        \
[end]     [end]

```
- the current is set to root.
- we iterate over the word cats.
    - ch = c, current = [c d], so we update current to the pointer stored at index of c.
    - ch = a, current = [a], so we update current to the pointer stored at index of a.
    - ch = t, current = [l t], so we update current to the pointer stored at index of t.
    - ch = s, current = [[s], end], we update the current to the pointer stored at index of s.
    - current = [end]
the end in the current is true, so the word exists in the trie.

Inspect the code editor to see how to search in the trie.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
